revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=築山古墳 |所在地=奈良県北葛城郡広陵町赤部 |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長200m、後円部径117m、高さ19m、前方部幅118m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=埴輪 |特記事項=陵墓参考地 }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）とは、[[奈良県]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある大型[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の一つ。
==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。
南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に造り出しが存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は水田、畑の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる。
墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の円筒埴輪から、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に勾玉、管玉、棗玉などが所蔵されている。
==参考文献== : 河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026 新泉社 2006年
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=築山古墳 |所在地=奈良県北葛城郡広陵町赤部 |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長200m、後円部径117m、高さ19m、前方部幅118m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=埴輪 |特記事項=陵墓参考地 }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）とは、[[奈良県]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある大型[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の一つ。

墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の円筒埴輪から、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に勾玉、管玉、棗玉などが所蔵されている。
==参考文献== * 河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026 新泉社 2006年

revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:05 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=新木山古墳 |所在地=奈良県北葛城郡広陵町赤部 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|2.7|N|135|44|14.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=埴輪 |被葬者=[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]（[[宮内庁]]推定） |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定（三吉陵墓参考地） |特記事項= }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の一つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は水田、畑の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の円筒埴輪から、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に勾玉、管玉、棗玉などが所蔵されている。 ==規模== * 墳丘長200m * 後円部径117m * 高さ19m * 前方部幅118m ==参考文献== * 河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:広陵町]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Nikiyama-kofun.jpg|250px]]
全景（右が後円部） |名称=新木山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|2.7|N|135|44|14.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m |築造年代=[[5世紀]]前半 |出土品=[[埴輪]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]] |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「三吉陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]が、外提に接して造られている。 ==規模== * 墳丘長200m * 後円部径117m * 高さ19m * 前方部幅118m ==脚注== {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:広陵町]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

revision.comment: 堺市ホームページの更新反映、および他の大型古墳とともに表記統一。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Nikiyama-kofun.jpg|250px]]
全景（右が後円部） |名称=新木山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|2.67|N|135|44|14.37|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=新木山古墳（三吉陵墓参考地）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m |築造年代=[[5世紀]]前半 |出土品=[[埴輪]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]] |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「三吉陵墓参考地」 |特記事項=全国第35位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2015年8月16日更新版）。 }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]（県指定史跡）が、外提に接して造られている。 ==規模== * 墳丘長200m * 後円部径117m * 高さ19m * 前方部幅118m ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ==外部リンク== [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26&frmCd=3-0-0-0-0 観光 古墳] - 広陵町 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Nikiyama-kofun.jpg|250px]]
全景（右が後円部） |名称=新木山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 2.67 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 14.37 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m |築造年代=[[5世紀]]前半 |出土品=[[埴輪]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]] |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「三吉陵墓参考地」 |特記事項=全国第35位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2015年8月16日更新版）。 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東 }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]（県指定史跡）が、外提に接して造られている。 ==規模== * 墳丘長200m * 後円部径117m * 高さ19m * 前方部幅118m ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ==外部リンク== [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26&frmCd=3-0-0-0-0 観光 古墳] - 広陵町 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Nikiyama-kofun.jpg|250px]]
全景（右が後円部） |名称=新木山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 2.67 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 14.37 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m |築造年代=[[5世紀]]前半 |出土品=[[埴輪]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「三吉陵墓参考地」 |特記事項=全国第36位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東 }} '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]（県指定史跡）が、外提に接して造られている{{Cite web |format=PDF |url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf |title=三吉石塚古墳・新木山古墳位置図 |publisher=広陵町 |accessdate=2017-12-25}}。 ==規模== * 墳丘長200m * 後円部径117m * 高さ19m * 前方部幅118m ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ==外部リンク== [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{国土航空写真}}]] '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]（県指定史跡）が、外提に接して造られている{{Cite web |format=PDF |url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf |title=三吉石塚古墳・新木山古墳位置図 |publisher=広陵町 |accessdate=2017-12-25}}。 ==規模== * 墳丘長200m * 後円部径117m * 高さ19m * 前方部幅118m ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{Otheruses||奈良県大和郡山市にある古墳（郡山陵墓参考地）|郡山新木山古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |名称=新木山古墳 |所属=[[馬見古墳群]]（中央群） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 2.67 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 14.37 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m（後円部） |築造時期=[[5世紀]]前半 |出土品=[[埴輪]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「三吉陵墓参考地」 |特記事項=全国第36位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東 }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|三吉陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（中央群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]（県指定史跡）が、外提に接して造られている{{Cite web |format=PDF |url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf |title=三吉石塚古墳・新木山古墳位置図 |publisher=広陵町 |accessdate=2017-12-25}}。 ==構造== [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{Center|空中写真（1979年）}}{{small| {{国土航空写真}}。}}]] * 墳丘長：200メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：117メートル ** 高さ：19メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：118メートル File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘（南東より） File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|墳丘（南西より） File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|墳丘（北より） File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|後円部側周濠 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[三吉石塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Japanese Kofun |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun Location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ** Diameter: 117 meters ** Height: 19 meters * Front Section ** Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]** {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:新木山古墳]][[ja:新木山古墳]] 2025

{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Japanese Kofun |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun Location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ** Diameter: 117 meters ** Height: 19 meters * Front Section ** Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]** {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:新木山古墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] 2025

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Arakiyama Kofun]] to [[Nikiyama Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Japanese Kofun |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun Location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:新木山古墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] 2025

{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] 2025

On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] 2025

{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] 2025

{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] -
