{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Mausoleum References]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Information: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information revision.text:
{{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Tumulus (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Tumulus (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Imperial Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |group={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude=34|latitude_min=33|latitude_sec=2.67 |longitude=135|longitude_min=44|longitude_sec=14.37 |ISO=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |dimensions=Tumulus length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |construction_period=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |excavated_items={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |buried_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}} "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference" |notable_features=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |icon=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference Worship Site}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Imperial Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. Excavated items preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-scale zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|format=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location Map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == Structure == [[File:7909qq Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Tumulus length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: qq7 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: qq8 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun,
