revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Kofun navbar}}" to "[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Mausoleum References]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Nikiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Mausoleum References]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Nikiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nikiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Mausoleum References]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nikiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Mausoleum References]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]]

This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:新木山古墳 up to rev 98082715
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||奈良県大和郡山市にある古墳（郡山陵墓参考地）|郡山新木山古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |名称=新木山古墳 |所属=[[馬見古墳群]]（中央群） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部字新木山 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 2.67 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 14.37 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長200m
高さ19m（後円部） |築造時期=[[5世紀]]前半 |出土品=[[埴輪]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「三吉陵墓参考地」 |特記事項=全国第36位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東 }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|三吉陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''新木山古墳'''（にきやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]赤部にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（中央群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''三吉陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第30代[[敏達天皇]]皇子[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]][[舒明天皇]]御父）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 ==概要== [[宮内庁]]が三吉[[陵墓参考地]]として管理しているため、一般市民は立入ることは出来ない。 南北走向の低位丘陵から東へ派生する小支丘を利用して築造されている。墳丘の主軸はおおむね東西にあり、両くびれ部に[[造り出し]]が存在する。その形態は[[巣山古墳]]のものに似ている。濠の幅は後円部で18メートル、前方部で22メートルほどである。外堤は[[水田]]、[[畑]]の畦畔として残っており、幅約20メートル前後、高さは3メートル以上あったと考えられる河上邦彦 『大和葛城の大古墳群・馬見古墳群』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026、新泉社、2006年。 墳丘内に埴輪列が存在することが確かめられている。またこの古墳の出土遺物として宮内庁に[[勾玉]]、[[管玉]]、[[棗]][[玉]]などが所蔵されている。採集された方形透かしと有黒斑の[[円筒埴輪]]から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と考えられる。馬見古墳群中央群では最後の大型前方後円墳である「大和の古墳Ⅰ」73頁。 後円部西側には、主軸を同じくする[[帆立貝形古墳]]の[[三吉石塚古墳]]（県指定史跡）が、外提に接して造られている{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf |title=三吉石塚古墳・新木山古墳位置図 |publisher=広陵町 |accessdate=2017-12-25}}。 ==構造== [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{Center|空中写真（1979年）}}{{small| {{国土航空写真}}。}}]] * 墳丘長：200メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：117メートル ** 高さ：19メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：118メートル File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘（南東より） File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|墳丘（南西より） File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|墳丘（北より） File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|後円部側周濠 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[三吉石塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にきやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{citation needed|Nikiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新木山古墳|version=98082715|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Mausoleum References]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]]
