This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakatsuragi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakatsuragi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新木山古墳|version=98082715|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Mausoleum References]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]]

Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakatsuragi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新木山古墳|version=98082715|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]]

Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakatsuragi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新木山古墳|version=98082715|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]]

Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Interred_individual=(As estimated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakatsuragi District|ja|北葛城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakatsuragi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. This {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}}. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}}. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}}, tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference Site|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:陵墓参考地]] to [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新木山古墳|version=98082715|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nikiyama Kofun]] [[ja:新木山古墳]] [[sn:ikiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]]

Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun template and pages that transclude it
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The kofun in Yamatokōriyama, Nara Prefecture (Kōriyama Mausoleum Reference)|Koriyama Arakiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |name=Nikiyama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group) |location=Akabe Aza Nikiyama, [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=2.67 |coordinates=135|coordinates=44|coordinates=14.37 |omitted=JP-29 |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area=Kofun length: 200 m
Height: 19 m (rear circular section) |built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Interred_individual=(As estimated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] |mausoleum={{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference" |notes=36th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Rankings (Nationwide)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018). |map=Japan Nara |type=Zenpō-kōen-fun-East }} [[File:Nikiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Nikiyama Kofun''' (新木山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. This [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Central Group). The actual interred individual is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages the site as the "Mitsuyoshi Mausoleum Reference," associating it with Prince Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe, the son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]. == Overview == As the Mitsuyoshi {{ill|Mausoleum Reference|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum Reference|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}, the site is under the management of the Imperial Household Agency and is not accessible to the general public. The kofun was built on a small ridge extending eastward from a lower north-south ridge. Its main axis runs approximately east-west, with {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} features at both constriction areas. The structure resembles the nearby [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The moat is approximately 18 meters wide at the rear circular section and 22 meters wide at the front section. The outer embankment, used as field ridges for rice paddies and farmland, is believed to have been about 20 meters wide and over 3 meters high. Kawakami Kunihiko, *"The Large Kofun Groups of Yamato-Katsuragi: The Umami Kofun Group"*, Shinseisha, 2006. A line of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] was confirmed on the tumulus. excavations preserved by the Imperial Household Agency include [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tubular beads, and natama (small beads). Cylindrical haniwa with square perforations and black mottling date the construction to the early 5th century. This kofun is the final large-map_size zenpō-kōen-fun of the Umami Kofun Group's Central Group. "Yamato no Kofun I," p. 73. On the west side of the rear circular section lies {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} aligned along the same axis and constructed adjacent to the outer embankment. {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/26/mitsuyoshiishidukakohun.pdf|title=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun & Nikiyama Kofun location map|publisher=Kōryō Town|accessdate=2017-12-25}} == burial == [[File:790911 Nikiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Aerial view (1979)}}{{small| {{ill|Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] * Kofun length: 200 meters * Rear Circular Section ''' Diameter: 117 meters ''' Height: 19 meters * Front Section ''' Width: 118 meters File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun (view from southeast) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|Kofun (view from southwest) File:Nikiyama Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|Kofun (view from north) File:Nikiyama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat on the rear circular section side == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nikiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 Tourist Intypeation: Kofun] - Kōryō Town official website {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun built, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) -
